apps.name: Property Manager V1
apps.summary: Maintenance command center for small landlords: intake, dispatch, updates, and issue history.
apps.reviewGate: Jeff verdict: NOT SIGN-OFF READY. Real browser QA confirmed the app is out of demo mode and persistence is real, but core request-detail workflows still break under normal use.
apps.nextStep: Fix the two blocking defects, then re-run manual browser QA. Blocker 1: image upload path fails on realistic file sizes and does not render previews correctly. Blocker 2: request-detail actions fail because lib/request-detail-actions.ts violates Next.js 'use server' export rules, breaking status changes, vendor assignment, and likely comments.
notes: Mario: the right wedge is a maintenance command center, not an all-in-one property management suite.
Barry Bot: Core multi-role workflow is live: operator flows, tenant submission/timeline/photos, vendor dispatch/queue/detail, and credential-based auth are in place.

Barry Bot: Current gate is no longer the auth-boundary harness; Bob fixed the hard-coded-port timeout issue in commit 06a6e79 and the teardown hang in commit aac70e0, so auth regression coverage now passes and exits cleanly.
Barry Bot: Product direction is now cleaner: operator pays, tenant/vendor access is free, invite-based onboarding is the path, and regions/urgency/expected completion/import-export are in scope.
Barry Bot: Jeff previously failed sign-off because the app could present convincing seed/demo reads when the DB was missing while real write flows were effectively broken.

Barry Bot: Bob moved the app out of demo mode into real persisted operation in commit cd4b7fd ('Wire SQLite for real DB persistence, exit demo mode'). Seed succeeded, demo banner is gone, and writes now persist at the app/Prisma layer.
Jeff: good progress, but call it what it is — this is a real persisted SQLite app, not completion of the original PostgreSQL-backed assignment. Manual browser QA is still the next honest gate.

Jeff: better now — the repo story matches reality, SQLite is declared instead of hidden, docs no longer lie, migrate status is clean, build is clean, and backend credibility is no longer the issue. The only remaining gap before full sign-off is real manual browser QA.

Jeff: final manual browser QA says not sign-off ready. Login, property creation, and unit creation passed, but maintenance request upload failed normal-use expectations due to a 1 MB body limit and broken image preview behavior, and request-detail actions failed with a Next.js 'use server' export violation in lib/request-detail-actions.ts.
